leagueBriefing: Ludvig Åberg is making the difficult TPC Sawgrass look like a playground, firing a 63 to take control of THE PLAYERS Championship while the world's best scrambled just to stay in the tournament.

games.headline: Åberg surges to two-shot lead at Sawgrass with record-tying front nine
games.brief: Ludvig Åberg looked every bit the rising superstar on Friday, carding a 9-under 63 to seize a two-shot lead at THE PLAYERS Championship. The Swede was unconscious early, going 5-under through his first four holes and tying the front-nine record at the Stadium Course with a 29. While Åberg made it look easy, the story at the other end of the leaderboard was Scottie Scheffler and Rory McIlroy sweatily grinding out pars just to make the cut, proving that Sawgrass still has teeth for everyone else.
bullets: Ludvig Åberg tied the TPC Sawgrass front-nine record with a 29, highlighted by hole-out chips on No. 4 and No. 9.
Xander Schauffele sits in solo second at 10-under after a bogey-free 65 where he hit a perfect 14-of-14 fairways.
World No. 1 Scottie Scheffler needed a clutch 8-foot birdie on the final hole to make the cut on the number at 1-over par.
Justin Thomas continued his bounce-back from injury, posting a second consecutive 68 to sit T4 at 8-under.
Cameron Young remains in the hunt at 9-under, seeking his second career PGA TOUR victory after breaking through last summer.
games.context: Åberg is in prime position to become the youngest PLAYERS champion since Si Woo Kim, while Scheffler’s streak of 69 consecutive cuts made narrowly survived.
